sections.paragraph:
fut. יִכְשַׁר-

(1) to be right, like the cognate roots אָשַׁר, יָשַׁר; followed by לִפְנֵי Est 8:5, וְכָשֵׁר הַדָּבָר לִפְנֵי הַמֶּלֶךְ “and (if) it be right before the king,” i.e. if it please the king. (Ch. id.)

(2) to prosper, to succeed; hence used of a seed, to sprout (Syr. id.), Ecc 11:6.

Hiphil, to cause to prosper, Ecc 10:10.

Hence כִּישֹׁור, כֹּושָׁרָה, and-
